# Design Plan: SBS Homes Landing Page

## Brief Summary

A single-page scroll-storytelling website for SBS Homes, an off-site timber home manufacturer. The page unfolds from emotional lifestyle experience to precision engineering underneath, targeting architects, developers, and potential buyers.

## Feature

- **Name**: landing-page
- **Route**: /

## Brand DNA

SBS Homes builds precision-manufactured timber homes off-site — not generic site-built houses. Founded by Adrian Farcut, they use advanced timber construction systems to deliver contemporary homes with factory-level precision.

The differentiator is engineering confidence: these homes are designed and built in controlled conditions, then assembled on-site. Every panel, every joint, every layer is specified and documented. The brochure itself reads like a technical manual wrapped in architectural photography — that's the brand.

The ONE thing a visitor remembers: these homes are precision-engineered off-site with advanced timber systems — not slapped together on a muddy building site.

Tone of voice: confident, precise, understated. Let the engineering speak. No superlatives, no marketing fluff. Think Apple "designed in California" energy — state what it is, show why it matters.

## Aesthetic Direction

Modern architectural editorial matching the brochure's sophisticated restraint. Clean, minimal, highly professional. Precision engineering that speaks through refined typography and careful spatial relationships. Generous whitespace with purposeful hierarchy.

## Anti-References

Not luxury real estate flash — no gold accents, no marble textures, no "exclusive lifestyle" language.
Not generic volume builder sites — no stock photo families, no "starting from £X" banners.
Not cluttered brochure layouts — no cramped grids, no competing CTAs.

## Inspiration Analysis

The brochure demonstrates refined typographic hierarchy with clean sans-serif fonts, sophisticated muted color palette dominated by warm greys and blacks, excellent whitespace usage, and minimal graphic elements. Very restrained, professional architectural presentation style.

### Mood Keywords

precision, editorial, confident, clean, minimal, architectural, understated, sophisticated

### Color Direction

Sophisticated muted palette from the brochure — warm greys, soft blacks, and cream tones create professional architectural authority. No bright accents needed. The light-to-dark progression IS the storytelling, using subtle tonal shifts rather than dramatic color contrasts.

### Typography Mood

- **Display**: Clean modern sans-serif matching the brochure's refined hierarchy — medium weight, excellent letterspacing. Weights: 400, 500.
- **Body**: Complementary sans-serif for clarity and professionalism — matching the brochure's text treatment. Weights: 400, 500.
- **Pairing**: Professional architectural restraint — both fonts work together to create the sophisticated, minimal aesthetic of the actual brochure.

## Sections

### Hero

- **Purpose**: Confident introduction — "These Are SBS Homes"
- **Tone / Mood**: Bold, architectural authority
- **Key elements**: Section number "1", full viewport presence, declarative headline

### Exterior Views

- **Purpose**: Street approach and architectural context
- **Tone / Mood**: Warm lifestyle invitation with editorial sophistication
- **Key elements**: Animation placeholder — headline only, empty content area

### Interior Lifestyle

- **Purpose**: Living experience inside these homes
- **Tone / Mood**: Intimate yet sophisticated domestic life
- **Key elements**: Animation placeholder — headline only, empty content area

### Floor Plans

- **Purpose**: General arrangement and room schedule
- **Tone / Mood**: Practical clarity, architectural documentation
- **Key elements**: Ground floor plan, first floor plan, room schedule

### Section Divider

- **Purpose**: Transition from lifestyle to engineering — "What Makes Them Special?"
- **Tone / Mood**: Pivot moment, building anticipation
- **Key elements**: Section number "2", background shift from light to dark

### Construction Overview

- **Purpose**: Exploded view of off-site timber panel system
- **Tone / Mood**: Engineering confidence, technical intrigue
- **Key elements**: Animation placeholder — headline only, empty content area

### Assembly Sequence

- **Purpose**: 7-step build process from slab to roof
- **Tone / Mood**: Methodical precision, controlled construction
- **Key elements**: Animation placeholder — headline only, empty content area

### Structural Floor Plans

- **Purpose**: Color-coded component specification
- **Tone / Mood**: Technical authority, brochure-level detail
- **Key elements**: External walls, internal walls, party walls mapping

### Component Details

- **Purpose**: Layer-by-layer specifications for each building element
- **Tone / Mood**: Deep technical dive, manufacturing precision
- **Key elements**: Ground floor slab, external walls, internal walls, party walls, intermediate floor, roof

### Footer

- **Purpose**: Direct contact with Adrian Farcut
- **Tone / Mood**: Accessible, personal after the technical journey
- **Key elements**: Phone, email, website, personal contact approach

## UX Flow

Three-act scroll narrative with visual transition from light warm backgrounds (lifestyle) to dark charcoal (engineering). Sticky navigation tracks progress through acts. Section numbers guide reader through the story progression.

## Content & Layout

- **Language**: English
- **Copy**: Real copy from brochure where available, placeholder English text where needed
- **Images**: Unsplash placeholders
- **Layout**: Mobile-first (default)
- **Constraints**: Animation-ready sections (Exterior Views, Interior Lifestyle, Construction Overview, Assembly Sequence) must be empty containers with headline only. Static sections (Floor Plans, Structural Plans, Component Details, Footer) fully built.

## Mockup Guidance

- **Mode**: B
- **Count**: 2
- **Variation direction**: Same architectural editorial style with different pacing, typography hierarchy, or section composition approaches.
